summary.intent: Address a fifth round of Codex automated review findings on PR #694 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically three new RatingGroup accessibility and behavior bugs identified after the fourth-pass fix was pushed.
summary.outcome: All three RatingGroup findings were fixed (fractional pointer intent value helper, non-divisor step max reachability, whole-mode arrow key roving focus), tests added, cargo xci-fast passed all 10 gates, and the fifth-pass fix was committed and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: The project uses a multi-pass Codex review workflow: fixes are committed, pushed, threads replied-to and resolved, then '@codex review' is re-triggered to start the next pass.
cargo xci-fast is the required pre-push gate with 10 sequential steps: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter stages.
Codex review reactions signal state: 'eyes' = reviewing in progress, no reaction = idle. Unresolved threads from chatgpt-codex-connector indicate pending findings.
The snapshot-reviewed label is applied via 'gh pr edit --add-label snapshot-reviewed' after each fix pass.
RatingGroup has distinct whole-mode vs fractional-mode behaviors that require separate aria and state machine handling.

code.finding: RatingGroup fractional mode lacked a value-based hover/rate intent helper; pointer intent needed to map directly to a value, not just a threshold.
Non-divisor step values could make max unreachable; needed clamping logic to ensure the maximum rating value is always accessible.
Whole-mode arrow key handling committed the new value but did not move the roving focus index to the newly checked radio item.
Proptest state machine for data_display contains RatingGroup coverage; new regression cases for the three findings were added here alongside unit tests.

workflow: Polling for async CI/review tools (Codex) via sleep + gh api in a loop is effective but slow; three 120-second poll cycles were needed before results appeared.
The review-fix workflow explicitly requires failing tests to be added before patching the implementation ('add failing coverage first, then patch and verify').
cargo +nightly fmt --all must be run after apply_patch changes since the formatter may apply additional line-wrapping changes beyond what patches specify.
GraphQL mutation resolveReviewThread requires the internal PRRT_ thread ID, not the comment database ID; these must be fetched separately before resolving.

friction: Codex review took multiple 120-second polling cycles (6+ minutes) before returning results, requiring repeated manual sleep-poll loops.
The automated review produces findings incrementally across multiple passes, requiring a full fix-push-retrigger cycle for each pass rather than resolving all issues at once.
Thread resolution requires two separate API calls per thread: one REST call to post a reply comment, one GraphQL mutation to mark resolved.
open_items: PR #694 is still open and pending another Codex review pass after the fifth-pass fix commit; approval has not yet been confirmed.

The proptest state machine data_display tests were extended but the full proptest suite runtime was not shown — coverage completeness for the new RatingGroup edge cases (non-divisor steps, fractional intent) should be verified.

The six issues resolved in the fourth pass (TagGroup focus, RemoveTag selection events, RatingGroup aria-checked, TagGroup aria-selected, RatingGroup slider pattern, TagGroup aria-live) represent accumulated tech debt that required four review passes to surface — a broader accessibility audit pass may be warranted.
